thesis: ONE geometry pass makes every surface aware of its borders — not a per-surface offset. Children clip to their parent (intersect), transient surfaces clamp to the work-area (clamp_inside), submenus flip at the screen edge (submenu_rect). The hit-region and the painted surface share one rect, so a hover/focus outline can never bleed past a panel border or off-screen.

panel.note: requested x=1750 → right=2030 overflows; clamp_inside shifts (not shrinks) left

submenu.note: flush-right would reach 2060 > 1912 → FLIP to the left side, then clamp

blob_backing.note: union(panel, submenu) then inflate(6) — stays fully on-screen because both children are clamped

row_focus_ring.note: a row hit/focus rect intersected with the panel content box — clipped exactly to the border, never past it
